reasoning: The target threshold temperature based on the 2025-10-26 data is 8.725°C. Bourges has a temperate climate where average temperatures fall consistently through autumn and into winter. The climatological daily mean for early November (Nov 2) remains around 9-10°C, meaning there is a slightly better than even chance (~55%) of exceeding 8.725°C. By late November (Nov 25), normal means drop to roughly 6-7°C, making it necessary to have mildly warm Atlantic weather patterns to exceed the threshold, lowering the probability to roughly 32%. For late January (Jan 24), the historical mean is typically around 4-5°C, meaning a daily average above 8.725°C requires a significantly warmer-than-average winter day, placing the probability around 24%. Uncertainty reflects standard climate variance for central France over these horizons.
